title: Anti-Corruption Bureau Officer
description: Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) Officers investigate and prosecute cases of corruption, bribery, and other financial crimes within government and public sectors. They work to uphold integrity and transparency in governance.
content: ACB Officers require strong investigative skills, a deep understanding of legal procedures, and unwavering integrity. They gather evidence, conduct raids, interrogate suspects, and prepare cases for prosecution. This career demands ethical conduct, courage, and a commitment to justice. Many enter through competitive exams conducted by state public service commissions or through internal promotions within law enforcement.

financialFeasibility.note: While the initial investment in education and preparation for competitive exams can be significant, the job itself offers good financial stability and benefits, making it accessible to middle-class families.

sectorScore.remarks: This is almost exclusively a government sector career. Opportunities in the private sector are limited to roles like forensic accounting or compliance, which are related but distinct.

relatedCareers.reason: Shares similar skills in investigation, law enforcement, and maintaining order.
relatedCareers.title: Police Officer

relatedCareers.reason: Provides legal expertise and represents the state in prosecuting corruption cases.
relatedCareers.title: Lawyer

relatedCareers.reason: Uncovers corruption and wrongdoing, often leading to investigations by the ACB.
relatedCareers.title: Investigative Journalist

genderDistribution.note: While traditionally male-dominated, there's an increasing emphasis on recruiting female officers into the ACB to promote diversity and inclusivity.
